title: Maps, Strings, Bytes, Runes, Unicode, and Deterministic Results · Runtime Recall
summary: An incident summarizer must count labels, normalize text, preserve UTF-8, and publish stable output despite unordered maps. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a Unicode-safe deterministic incident summarizer after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Create, update, delete, clear, and query maps while distinguishing missing keys from stored zero values.
Choose comparable key types and explain map reference behavior, iteration instability, mutation rules, and concurrency limits.
Treat strings as immutable byte sequences and convert to or from byte slices with explicit allocation and encoding intent.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Map Create Access · changed case · Review Map Create Access
steps.instruction:
review case — Incident labels include "café", "café", "東京", invalid UTF-8, and 4 repeated ASCII keys. Count without confusing missing and zero, report byte and rune evidence, then sort keys for deterministic output.
